body:
2026-04-18 dogfooding evidence: 2 omlx attempts at cheap tier, 2 no_changes.

Run 1 — ddx-91bc770a (Checkpoint dirty parent trees before managed bead execution):
- harness: agent / provider: vidar-omlx / model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit
- duration: 1300s (21.7 min)
- tokens: 254,413
- outcome: task_no_changes — no rationale written
- bead-class: substantial git-layer refactor

Run 2 — ddx-e3493010 (Resolution Path developer guide):
- harness: agent / provider: vidar-omlx / model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit
- duration: 4150s (69.2 min)
- tokens: 2,086,838 (2.09M)
- outcome: task_no_changes — no rationale written
- bead-class: documentation-only markdown authoring with code-walk citations

Findings:

1. v0.3.14 SSE filter is solid. 2M+ tokens streamed on run 2 with zero parse errors. Cheap-tier dispatch path no longer the blocker.

2. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit is not strong enough to reliably close real DDx beads. Both attempts burned significant wall-clock and tokens without committing. "Free" local compute has real opportunity cost when model thrashes for an hour without output.

3. Without ladder escalation, operator has to manually fall back. This IS the motivating case for ddx-bbb65768: default profile should detect no_changes on first tier and escalate to standard (codex subscription) for a second try.

4. AC #5 of this bead sets 50% close-at-cheap target over 10 representative beads. Current observed rate: 0% close-at-cheap over 2 representative beads. Epic delivery must LIFT this rate — either through better capability gating OR by making escalation cheap enough that failed cheap attempts don't dominate total cost.

5. Qwen3-Coder-Next-MLX-4bit might do better on code-heavy beads; gemma-4-31B-it on docs. Worth follow-up experiment pinning different local models per bead-class after autorouting foundation lands.

Evidence bundles: .ddx/executions/20260418T023901-ba3d8d59/ and 20260418T030326-*/.
summary: Qwen3.6 cheap-tier: 2 no_changes over 2 beads (21m + 69m, 2.34M tokens, 0 commits)
